changed_signals.de_escalation_watch: SIGNAL: Iran easing Hormuz restrictions per maritime data — countries cutting individual deals instead of joining US coalition. This is a partial de-escalation on the economic front, though military strikes continue. Europe refuses Hormuz naval deployment. Putin-Trump call today is the key diplomatic variable for Ukraine. Zero ceasefire signals in ME active theaters.

latest_news.headline: Iran strikes Qatar gas facility after South Pars attack — Gulf energy war widens
latest_news.text: Iran strikes a major Qatar gas facility in retaliation for Israeli strike on South Pars. Saudi Arabia intercepted 4 ballistic missiles targeting Riyadh — fragment fell near refinery south of city. UAE air defenses responding to new missile/drone threats. Gulf energy war now fully active.

latest_news.headline: Gabbard confirms IC assessment: Iran nuclear enrichment 'obliterated' in last year's strikes
latest_news.text: DNI Gabbard reiterates at Senate hearing: intelligence community assessed Iran's nuclear enrichment program was 'obliterated' by air strikes last summer. Significant de-escalation signal on nuclear dimension — program already destroyed before current conflict.

latest_news.headline: Iran FM Araghchi: nuclear doctrine 'not likely to change' — new Supreme Leader yet to weigh in
latest_news.text: FM Araghchi states Iran's stance against nuclear weapons won't significantly change. New Supreme Leader Mojtaba has not yet publicly expressed his view. De-escalation signal on nuclear threshold despite conventional war raging.

latest_news.headline: Russia financially benefiting from Iran war — US issues sanctions waiver for Russian oil
latest_news.text: Treasury issues 30-day waiver allowing Russia to sell oil already loaded on tankers. Analysts say Moscow is clear financial beneficiary as Hormuz disruptions spike energy prices. Bessent calls it 'narrowly tailored' — analysts and Russia disagree.

latest_news.headline: Europe refuses Hormuz involvement: 'Not our war'
latest_news.text: European leaders reject direct involvement in US-Israeli military operations against Iran. Germany, France, UK cite unpredictable conflict with unclear aims. Trump's Hormuz coalition request gets zero European commitments.

outlook: CRITICAL (Day 24, 20:04 UTC) — (1) Iran strikes Qatar gas facility + Saudi intercepts 4 missiles near Riyadh refinery — Gulf energy war widening; (2) UAE air defenses under new missile/drone threat; (3) Gabbard: Iran nuclear enrichment 'obliterated' last summer — nuclear threshold lowered; (4) FM Araghchi says nuclear doctrine unchanged; (5) Europe refuses Hormuz: 'Not our war'; (6) Russia financially benefiting from war via sanctions waiver; (7) Fed holds rates citing war uncertainty. Military escalation continues; nuclear de-escalation signal notable.
